Title: Divergent fatty acid desaturase 2 is essential for falcarindiol biosynthesis in carrot

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: fatty acid desaturase 2 / D12-acetylenase with dual D14-desaturase-associated activity
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Falcarindiol (falcarin-type polyacetylene) biosynthesis via the crepenynate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Crepenynic acid/dehydrocrepenynic acid precursor branch of falcarindiol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Linoleic acid-derived fatty acid substrate leading to crepenynic acid
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Crepenynic acid; dehydrocrepenynic acid in certain combinatorial contexts
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of crepenynic acid as a D12-acetylenase; also contributes to desaturation toward dehydrocrepenynic acid and can participate in production of an unidentified PUFA depending on FAD2 partner combinations
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms essential precursor pool for falcarindiol and helps channel flux toward immediate precursor dehydrocrepenynic acid
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression positively correlated with falcarindiol accumulation; associated with root periderm
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Young root periderm-associated expression correlation with falcarindiol
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Constitutive accumulation in roots under normal/sterile growth; greenhouse-grown plants; hairy root culture for validation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Association analysis; RNA-seq/metabolite correlation; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; combinatorial expression assays; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout in carrot hairy roots; overexpression in carrot hairy roots; LC-MS and GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Functional combinatorial partners DcFAD2-19; DcFAD2-12; DcFAD2-18; DcFAD2-17; DcFAD2-13
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential positive regulator of falcarindiol accumulation; knockout of DcFAD2-1/DcFAD2-9 strongly reduced falcarindiol, while DcFAD2-1 overexpression increased falcarindiol, DCA, and PUFA levels
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DcFAD2-1 was identified by expression-metabolite correlation as a candidate falcarindiol gene. 2. Transient expression showed DcFAD2-1 is a new D12-acetylenase producing crepenynic acid and, in combinations, redirects flux to DCA or PUFA. 3. CRISPR knockout of DcFAD2-1 together with the closely related DcFAD2-9 sharply reduced falcarindiol. 4. Overexpression of DcFAD2-1 increased falcarindiol and precursor-related unusual fatty acids, supporting hub-gene status.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuous activity redirects flux to unidentified PUFA branch; possible off-target metabolic shifts among falcarin branches
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target hub gene for breeding or engineering elevated falcarindiol/falcarins; useful for synthetic biology but should consider combinatorial partner effects and branch redirection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located in array 3; showed the highest catalytic activity among tested genes in N. benthamiana; array 3 genes were previously uncharacterized and emerged as central hub genes
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Overexpression vector pK7WG2R promoter not specified in text

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Falcarindiol biosynthesis via the crepenynate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Linoleic acid-derived fatty acid substrate leading to crepenynic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as a newly identified D12-acetylenase producing crepenynic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms essential precursor for falcarindiol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Part of hub-gene module in array 3
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly correlated with DcFAD2-1 and falcarindiol accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; co-targeted CRISPR-Cas9 knockout with DcFAD2-1 in carrot hairy roots; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive contributor essential within the DcFAD2-1/DcFAD2-9 hub module; loss together with DcFAD2-1 markedly reduced falcarindiol
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DcFAD2-9 was cloned as an array 3 gene highly correlated with DcFAD2-1. 2. Tobacco assays identified it as a new D12-acetylenase. 3. Dual knockout with DcFAD2-1 caused strong falcarindiol reduction in hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stack/editing target together with DcFAD2-1 to modulate falcarindiol levels
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Closely related to DcFAD2-1 and exhibits similar catalytic activity

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Crepenynic acid; dehydrocrepenynic acid in combinatorial assays
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces crepenynic acid as a D12-acetylenase and, with DcFAD2-19, channels flux to dehydrocrepenynic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms precursor and contributes to immediate downstream precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in tobacco leaves for assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient individual and combinatorial expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Functional partner DcFAD2-19; also with DcFAD2-17 or DcFAD2-13 for PUFA production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes production of crepenynic acid and, with partners, dehydrocrepenynic acid or PUFA precursors relevant to falcarindiol pathway branching
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DcFAD2-6 is a D12-acetylenase correlated with falcarindiol. 2. Alone it increases crepenynic acid. 3. With DcFAD2-19 it shifts metabolism toward DCA. 4. With DcFAD2-17 or -13 it redirects to unidentified PUFA.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuity and combinatorial redirection to non-falcarindiol branches
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supplying enzyme for pathway reconstruction, but combinatorial context is critical
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously characterized and functionally conserved with DcFAD2-7

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Crepenynic acid; dehydrocrepenynic acid in combinatorial assays
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Primarily produces crepenynic acid as a D12-acetylenase; when co-expressed with DcFAD2-19, DcFAD2-12, or DcFAD2-18, metabolic flux is redirected toward dehydrocrepenynic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms precursor and contributes to immediate downstream precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in tobacco leaves for assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient individual and combinatorial expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Functional partners DcFAD2-19; DcFAD2-12; DcFAD2-18; DcFAD2-17; DcFAD2-13
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces crepenynic acid and serves as a combinatorial node that can redirect flux to DCA or unidentified PUFA branches
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DcFAD2-7 alone mainly produces crepenynic acid. 2. Co-expression with DcFAD2-19 drastically decreases CA and increases DCA. 3. Similar synergistic effects occur with DcFAD2-12 and -18. 4. With DcFAD2-17 or -13 it contributes to PUFA formation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Branch redirection may reduce CA accumulation and divert flux to other unusual fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for synthetic pathway assembly with partner selection to tune branch outcome
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously characterized D12-acetylenase; phylogenetically related to parsley ELI12

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Crepenynic acid; minor dehydrocrepenynic acid-related product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts as a D12-acetylenase producing crepenynic acid and also shows dual functionality yielding desaturated CA matching DCA spectra
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms essential precursor for falcarindiol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in tobacco leaves for assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces crepenynic acid precursor and contributes to precursor diversification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DcFAD2-8 is a characterized D12-acetylenase. 2. Transient expression increased crepenynic acid in tobacco leaves. 3. It also showed dual activity producing a desaturated CA species matching DCA spectra.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate precursor enzyme for falcarin engineering

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Crepenynic acid-derived intermediate produced by partner FAD2s
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as a D14-desaturase in combinatorial context, converting flux from crepenynic acid-producing partners toward dehydrocrepenynic acid; alone produced neither CA nor DCA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: One of the correlated candidate FAD2 genes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in tobacco leaves for assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Redirects metabolic flux from CA toward DCA when paired with CA-producing FAD2s, supporting falcarindiol precursor biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DcFAD2-19 alone did not yield CA or DCA. 2. Co-expression with DcFAD2-7, -6, or -1 strongly promoted DCA accumulation while decreasing CA. 3. Therefore it functions as a D14-desaturase required in precursor channeling.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Depends on partner enzymes; may reduce CA pool by redirecting flux
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful midstream enzyme for engineered DCA/falcarindiol precursor production when paired with D12-acetylenases
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously implicated in Arabidopsis co-expression assays and confirmed here in tobacco

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Crepenynic acid-derived intermediate from DcFAD2-7 or related partners
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts in combination with DcFAD2-7 to promote DCA production, consistent with D14-desaturase activity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor pool for falcarindiol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Synergistically promotes DCA production with DcFAD2-7
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DcFAD2-12 was identified as a new D14-desaturase. 2. It showed synergistic effects with DcFAD2-7 leading to DCA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful combinatorial component for precursor engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of six new D14-desaturases reported

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Crepenynic acid-derived intermediate from DcFAD2-7 or related partners
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts in combination with DcFAD2-7 to promote DCA production, consistent with D14-desaturase activity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor pool for falcarindiol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Synergistically promotes DCA production with DcFAD2-7
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DcFAD2-18 was identified as a new D14-desaturase. 2. It showed synergistic effects with DcFAD2-7 leading to DCA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful combinatorial component for precursor engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of six new D14-desaturases reported

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: fatty acid desaturase 2 / divergent FAD2 producing unidentified PUFA branch intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Falcarin network branch related to falcarindiol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: In combination with DcFAD2-1, -6, or -7, redirects metabolism from CA/DCA branch to an unidentified PUFA branch; alone did not produce detectable CA, DCA, or PUFA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with falcarindiol precursor branch by diverting flux to alternative unusual fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Redirects pathway flux into unidentified PUFA rather than the canonical DCA branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DcFAD2-17 alone had no detectable product. 2. Combined with DcFAD2-1, -6, or -7, it promoted an unknown PUFA. 3. This supports pathway branching and complexity in the falcarin network.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Diverts metabolic flux away from falcarindiol precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important cautionary enzyme when engineering falcarindiol because it may cause unwanted branch products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not directly tied to falcarindiol accumulation genetically, it is a core experimental gene defining branch redirection

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: In combination with DcFAD2-1, -6, or -7, redirects metabolism from CA/DCA branch to an unidentified PUFA branch; alone did not produce detectable CA, DCA, or PUFA

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DcFAD2-13 alone had no detectable product. 2. Combined with DcFAD2-1, -6, or -7, it promoted an unknown PUFA. 3. This supports pathway branching and complexity in the falcarin network.
